sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Nottingham Forest are in strong form, unbeaten in their last 5 matches with 3 wins, including a stunning 3-0 away victory over Liverpool. Brighton & Hove Albion have also shown consistency, with no losses in their last 3 games, securing 2 wins, including a 3-0 triumph over Leeds United. Both teams are displaying confidence ahead of this clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Nottingham Forest have dominated with a remarkable 7-0 win in February 2025, while the reverse fixture ended in a 0-0 draw. Forest have not lost to Brighton in their last 3 meetings, which could give them a psychological edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Brighton & Hove Albion, sitting 7th with 19 points, have scored 19 goals this season, showing attacking flair led by Danny Welbeck (7 goals). Nottingham Forest, in 16th with 12 points, have struggled offensively with just 13 goals but have overperformed their expected goals by 2.55, ranking 4th in the league for efficiency. Brighton's higher league position and goal tally suggest they might have the upper hand.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Forest will miss key midfielder Morgan Gibbs-White, which could impact their creativity despite his recent form (3 goals, 1 assist). Brighton have no major absences and boast players like Yankuba Minteh (4 assists, league-leading) and Welbeck, who could exploit Forest's defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Brighton's stronger league standing and attacking output, coupled with Forest's missing key player, the Sofascore prediction of a 42% chance for an away win seems plausible. However, Forest's recent form and home advantage at the City Ground, along with their head-to-head dominance, make this a tight contest. Expect a competitive match with Brighton slightly favored.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Brighton & Hove Albion lead Nottingham Forest 1-0 at the City Ground. The away side struck late in the first half through Maxim De Cuyper, capitalizing on a well-worked move just before the whistle. Forest showed intent but couldn’t convert their opportunities, missing a big chance, while Brighton made the most of their attacking precision despite also missing a notable opportunity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Nottingham Forest have been competitive in their approach, showing a decent share of possession and creating chances in the final third with multiple entries into the penalty area. However, their finishing has let them down, with a key miss proving costly. Defensively, they’ve held up reasonably well, winning a fair share of duels and aerial battles. Brighton, on the other hand, have dominated the ball and looked more clinical in attack, even if they haven’t fully maximized their opportunities. Their defense has been solid, limiting Forest’s clear-cut chances while maintaining composure under pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Forest started with purpose, pushing forward in the opening minutes before Brighton shifted the tide with a spell of sustained pressure. The home side regained control for a significant period midway through the half, showing attacking intent with several dangerous moments. However, Brighton wrestled back dominance as the half progressed, culminating in a crucial surge just before the break that led to their goal. Forest tried to respond late but couldn’t find a breakthrough.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at the break
halftimeSections.text: For Nottingham Forest, Nicolás Domínguez has been influential in midfield, creating a big chance and delivering key passes to spark attacks. Ibrahim Sangaré also stood out with strong defensive contributions and composure on the ball. On the Brighton side, Maxim De Cuyper was the difference-maker, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat with his involvement in build-up play. Yankuba Minteh has been lively as well, frequently involved in dangerous moves down the flank.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Nottingham Forest will likely come out with renewed energy, chasing an equalizer in front of their home crowd. They’ll need to sharpen their finishing and capitalize on entries into Brighton’s penalty area to turn the game around. Brighton, meanwhile, will aim to protect their lead while exploiting spaces on the counter. With their higher expected goals tally from the first half, they’ll be confident in adding to their scoreline if Forest push too hard. The game remains open, and tactical adjustments at the break could play a decisive role.
